description_long: This object is a large, classical-style obelisk structure made primarily of white marble with a rectangular, elongated body and a peaked, triangular pediment at the top. The obelisk is supported by a series of fluted columns with ornate bases and capitals, reminiscent of Greco-Roman architecture. The main body is elevated above the ground, with a smaller, attached platform extending from one side, also supported by columns. The surface of the obelisk is smooth and polished, with subtle gray veining typical of marble. There is a visible inscription in a stylized font on one side, and the overall design is symmetrical and monumental. The structure appears solid and heavy, suitable for placement in a public square or park.
description: A monumental white marble obelisk with fluted columns, a peaked pediment, and a side platform, featuring a visible inscription and classical architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the peaked pediment, central columns, and the attached side platform with its own columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the obelisk, the arrangement of columns, and the rectangular marble surfaces.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the symmetry of the columns and the triangular pediment, with the inscription visible in reverse.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side platform and the alignment of columns, with the inscription partially visible.
attribute_inscription: There is a visible inscription reading 'Justin Foust' in a stylized, serif font on the left side of the obelisk, rendered in English and clearly legible.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible beyond the fluted columns and classical pediment, which are architectural rather than decorative embellishments.
attribute_base_design: The base consists of a series of rectangular marble platforms supported by fluted columns with ornate bases, providing a raised and stable foundation.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a triangular pediment with a smooth marble finish, echoing classical temple architecture, without additional decoration.
